new_classes.label_en: Other fruit spirit in containers ≤ 2 L (subheading 2208 90 48)
new_classes.definition_en: Spirituous beverage obtained exclusively by alcoholic fermentation and distillation of fruit other than plums, pears or cherries (e.g. apricots, bilberries, raspberries, blackberries, black/white/red currants, strawberries, apples), presented in containers of 2 litres or less. Classified under CN subheading 2208 90 48.

new_classes.label_en: Other-fruit origin quality (spirit derived exclusively from fermented fruit other than plums, pears or cherries)
new_classes.label_de: Andere-Frucht-Ursprungsqualität (Branntwein ausschließlich aus vergärten Früchten außer Pflaumen, Birnen oder Kirschen)
new_classes.definition_en: Quality borne by a spirituous beverage attesting that it was obtained exclusively by alcoholic fermentation and distillation of fruit other than plums, pears or cherries, such as apricots, bilberries, raspberries, blackberries, currants, strawberries, or apples.

new_classes.label_en: Other-fruit fermented mash distillation process (fruit other than plum, pear or cherry)
new_classes.label_de: Destillationsprozess aus vergärtem Obstmaisch (andere Früchte als Pflaumen, Birnen oder Kirschen)
new_classes.definition_en: Distillation process applied to fermented mash of fruit other than plums, pears or cherries (e.g. apricots, bilberries, raspberries, blackberries, currants, strawberries, apples), constituting the production process for spirits classified under CN subheading 2208 90 48.

coverage_explanation: The proposed axioms capture the three main distinguishing criteria from the legal text: (1) the spirit must be obtained exclusively by alcoholic fermentation and distillation of fruit (modelled via OtherFruitMashDistillationProcess); (2) the fruit must not be plums, pears or cherries (modelled via OtherFruitOriginQuality, declared disjoint from PlumPearCherryFruitOriginQuality); (3) the container volume must be ≤ 2 L (modelled via SmallContainer part restriction). Coverage is not 1.0 because: (a) the enumeration of specific permitted fruit types (apricots, bilberries, raspberries, blackberries, currants, strawberries, apples) cannot be fully expressed as OWL class restrictions without a dedicated FruitType vocabulary that is not yet in the TBox; (b) the 'exclusively' qualifier on fermentation+distillation is captured semantically in the process class definition but not as a formal OWL restriction; (c) the legal text is truncated so further fruit examples may be missing.
